free_text: Optical model analyses of Li-6,7 Pb-208 elastic
scattering near the Coulomb barrier.
SERC, Daresbury Laboratory, Warrington.
Targets used were 100 mug/cm**2 Pb-208 vacuum deposited
onto a 10 mug/cm**2 carbon backing and 200
and 500 mug/cm**2 self supporting Pb-208 foils.
Tandem Van de Graaff accelerator of the
S.E.R.C. Daresbury Laboratory Nuclear Structure
Optical model analysis.
*By authors*.An overall normalization was obtained at
each energy by assuming that the monitor detectors were
measuring Rutherford scattering. This leads to an
estimated error in our absolute normalization of order

By CAJAD-figure 1 of J,NP/A,571,326,1994.
.The error bars shown are the statistical
counting error, which is the dominant source of error
using this method of data extraction.

*By authors*.The fusion cross sections are the results
of barrier-penetration model calculations and
non-elastic are results of optical potential parameters
reproducing Rutherford ratio.
